title: olearia ciliata var. squamifolia
intro: Olearia ciliata var. squamifolia, also known as the shrubby tree daisy or silver daisy bush, is a plant species native to eastern Australia, characterized by its silvery foliage and white daisy-like flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties that can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions. The health benefits of Olearia ciliata var. squamifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, protect against infection, and promote tissue repair, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or tincture, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
